sections.paragraph:
Χαρράν (Χαράν TestIss 1:5 Charles; Just., D. 58, 11; חָרָן. Gen 11:31f; 12:4f; 27:43; Philo; TestLevi 2:1), ἡ indecl. (Jos., Ant. 1, 152; 285* εἰς [τὴν] Χαρράν is surely acc. of Χαρρά) Haran, a place in Mesopotamia (=Κάρραι, Carrhae, famous for the defeat of Crassus that took place there in 53 b.c.), where Abraham lived for a time (Gen 11:31; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 18 Jac.) Ac 7:2, 4.—BHHW II 647.

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Charles Charles = RC., Revelation (ICC); also used in connection with Test12Patr—Lists 2, 6

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

acc. acc. = accusative

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1081
